sanskrit:
सन्न्यासस्तु महाबाहो दुःखमाप्तुमयोगतः।
योगयुक्तो मुनिर्ब्रह्म न चिरेणाधिगच्छति॥
transliteration: sannyāsas tu mahā-bāho duḥkham āptum ayogataḥ | yoga-yukto munir brahma na cireṇādhigacchati ||
literal_meaning: Renunciation, O mighty-armed, is hard to attain without yoga; the sage who is yoked in yoga reaches Brahman without delay.
contextual_meaning: Krishna warns Arjuna that merely dropping duties (pseudo-renunciation) increases inner conflict unless one simultaneously cultivates a disciplined, devotional mind-state; true release is psychological integration, not physical withdrawal.
psychological_state: Arjuna oscillates between fight, flight, and freeze; the verse reframes renunciation as mindful engagement rather than dissociation.
trigger_scenario: A high-achiever fantasizes about quitting everything to 'find peace,' but feels worse when idle—realizing escape is not healing.
anchor_text: Let go inwardly, not outwardly—peace comes through disciplined mind, not abandoned duty.
